violations.text: Stuffing was observed held at 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was observed double panned in the steam table. Per PIC, food was placed in the unit an hour prior to inspection. Food reheated to above 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. The following was observed: 1) Meatballs were placed hot in a bowl deeper than 4 inches and covered on the counter 2) Fried eggplant was observed cooling in a pan and covered on the counter Proper cooling methods reviewed with person in charge. Cooling methods guide left on site for staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
No person in charge that has a food safety certificate was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as leafy greens) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener moved to ware-washing area at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Chicken was observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. The chicken was observed being held in a double pan. Staff notified to keep chicken in the single hotel pan in the hot holding unit to maintain proper temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated to over 165 degrees Fahrenheit at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024
The gasket of the prep unit near the hot holding unit was observed with a frayed gasket. The door of the prep unit observed to be loose fitting. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the grease trap have an accumulation of grease. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024
